scope: V3 content parser and mapper service
Block type detection and conversion logic
assumptions: V3 questions/subquestions exist in a known format that can be parsed
Existing block types (RichText, Select, MultipleChoice, etc.) are available to map to
ExerciseAssets collection exists and supports the required asset types
The system has access to Local API with appropriate permissions for asset creation
review_questions: Should the mapping logic be implemented as a standalone service, a Payload hook, or a utility module?
What is the expected input format for V3 questions (JSON schema, raw text, specific API response)?
How should ambiguous content be handled - log warnings, raise errors, or use best-fit silently?
Is there a preferred naming convention for the new module (e.g., V3Mapper, QuestionParser, ContentSegmenter)?
complexity_reasoning: Scope: 22 (10+ block types, multiple modules needed for parser/mapper/segmenter). Risk: 16 (security-critical asset creation, ExerciseAssets access control). Novelty: 16 (entirely new mapping logic, no existing patterns). Cross-Domain: 12 (backend parsing + frontend block rendering + infra asset management). Ambiguity: 7 (implementation approach unclear, needs architect decision). Dependencies: 6 (depends on existing block types, ExerciseAssets collection, auth context). Total: 78

input_quality.reasoning: Input contains ## Core Behaviors & Requirements with 5 detailed requirements sections and ## Acceptance Criteria with 6 checkable items. Has clear technical context including security constraints. Promoted spec.md for gap analysis.
